description:
Fixes the cheap-first principle (only sci-mt had a smoke path). flow.run(smoke=2) truncates every fan-out (Flow.map etc.) to N items and runs the WHOLE DAG including analysis steps — exercising the full pipeline (train → sample → judge → plot) before the fleet launches, catching schema mismatches in plotting before the $200 of GPU time. Smoke outputs must be tagged/segregated so they never pollute real results or the memoization cache for real runs.

## Acceptance criteria
flow.run(smoke=N) runs every step of the DAG with fan-outs truncated to N; smoke artifacts are tagged and excluded from real-run cache keys and results; documented in README/cookbook.

## Notes
REVERSED per design feedback: engine smoke mode (PR #21) reverted in PR #22 (v1.8.0). Resolution is now the config-first pattern — workflows take a config.yaml and ship a *_smoke.yaml next to it; memo segregation is automatic (config values ride into step inputs). Documented in README 'Cheap-first: smoke configs, not an engine mode'.
title: Engine-level smoke mode: flow.run(smoke=N)
